State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance •• Administration •• Executive/Legal •• Enforcement
(916) 322-5662          322-5660              322-5901          322-6441

July 31, 1985

Douglas DeYoung, Treasurer
20th Assembly District Democratic Committee
888 Holly Road
Belmont, CA 94002
                                          Re: FPPC Advice No. A-85-150

Dear Mr. DeYoung:

     Your letter of January 31, 1985, addressed to the Secretary of
State's office was referred to the Fair Political Practices
Commission for response on July 2, 1985. I apologize for the delay
in responding to your questions.

     Government Code Section 84211(j) does require contributions of
$100 or more be itemized on campaign statements providing "...the
date of the contribution, the cumulative amount of contributions
made to a candidate, elected officer or committee, the full name of
the candidate..."

     However, the Commission's staff has determined that
contributions made to federal candidates are not required to be
itemized but rather may be disclosed in a lump sum with a notation
that the expenditure represents contributions to federal
candidates. The rationale for this is that federal candidates are
required under the federal election laws to disclose all
contributions they receive on FEC campaign forms which are also
filed with the Secretary of State's office. To require the
itemization pursuant to the Political Reform Act of contributions
of $100 or more made to federal candidates does not further the
intent of the Act because anyone wishing to see what contributions
a federal candidate has received can do so by looking at the FEC
campaign statements.

     The letters dated January 16 and 17, 1985, from the Secretary
of State's office are requesting that you amend the 450s filed by
the 20th Assembly District Democratic Committee because these
statements itemized contributions to federal candidates. I am
asking the Secretary of State's office to reconsider their request
for the amendments covered in the letters of January 16 and 17,
1985. In the future, however, if you wish, you may report
contributions to federal candidates in the manner described above.

Douglas DeYoung
July 31, 1985
Page 2


     Also, as a reminder, you are only required to itemize
contributions which cumulatively reach $100 within a calendar
year. On your statement filed August 1, 1984, covering the period
January 1 through June 30, 1984, you itemized two $50.00
contributions. Contributions under $100 may be reported on Line 2,
Part IV-Summary, Expenditures Under $100 (Not Itemized).

     If you have additional questions, please do not hesitate to
contact me.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Mary Ann Kvasager
                                        Political Reform Consultant
                                        Technical Assistance and Analysis
                                        Division

MAK:kt

cc:  Gloria Gilmore, Secretary
     of State's Office, Political
     Reform Division

TO: [ ]JEANNE          [ ] JAY      ( ) DAN                FROM:_____Kvasager_____

     [ ]LYNN            [ ] BARBARA ( ) JOHN K

     [ ]ROGER           [ ] CARLA   ( ) MARY ANN              DATE:_____1/31/85_____
                                                              (we received from SOS
     [ ]HELEN           [ ] OTHER______________              July 2, 1985.)

[ ]TELEPHONE ADVICE - SEE BELOW

[X]CORRESPONDENCE ADVICE - SEE ATTACHED DRAFT
     (IF YOU HAVE ANY COMMENTS, NOTIFY ADVISOR WITHIN 1 DAY)

FILE LOCATION:__________________________________________________________

INCLUDE IN ADVICE PACKAGE:        YES [ ]        NO [ ]


     CALLER: Douglas DeYoung, Treasurer

     REPRESENTING 20th Assembly Dist. Democratic Committee

     PHONE NUMBER:_________________________________


QUESTION: How to report contributions made by the 20th Assembly
          District Democratic Committee to federal candidates.




ANSWER:    Contributions to federal candidates are not required to

           be itemized but rather disclosed on Schedule E in a

           lump sum with a notation that the expenditures represent

           contributions to federal candidates.

RE: TWENTIETH ASSEMBLY DISTRICT DEMOCRATIC COMMITTEE ID# 801476


888 Holly Road
Belmont CA 94002
January 31, 1985

Ms. Marian Ash/Ms. Gloria Gilmore
Political Reform Division - Secretary of State
P.O. Box 1467
Sacramento, CA 95807

Dear Ms. Ash & Ms. Gilmore:

This acknowledges you letters of January 16 & 17
(copies attached) requesting amendments to my
campaign disclosure statements of June 28 and
October 23, 1984.

Your letter states in part:

    Contributions to candidates for federal office
    should be disclosed in a lump sum on Section I,
    "Expenditures and Contributions Made To:"

I have several questions, comments, and suggestions
concerning this.

1.  I cannot find the above statement in any of my
    reference documents pertaining to the preparation
    of the report. I'm not saying it doesn't appear
    I just can't find it. Please advise where it
    appears.

2.  The statement is inconsistent with the general
    rule that a transaction or series of transactions
    cumulating $100.00 or more in a calendar year with
    a given entity must be itemized. Why are you
    making this exception?

3.  I have been under the impression that transactions
    between committees should be itemized regardless of
    the amount involved. Most treasurers operate under
    this assumption.

4.  I have been under the impression that while treasurers
    must not fail to itemize those transactions which are
    required to be itemized, this does not mean that trea-
    surers must not itemize transactions which are not
    required to be itemized. We often desire to make in-
    formation available to the general public above and

beyond that required by law.  In this case the 20th
Assembly District Democratic Committee wishes to make
its financial support to Martin Carnoy a matter of
public record.  Treasurers often like to itemize con-
tributions of less than $100.00 in order to demonstrate
a broad base of local support or highlight some other
point.  I realize that there must be a practical limit
to this so that required itemized transactions are not
lost in a mass of trivial detail and reports do not
become too bulky, but most treasurers won't go to such
excesses anyway.

     5.  I'm not sure exactly what you are asking me to do in
this particular situation.  Your letters refer to
"candidates" (plural) for federal office.  There is
only one candidate (singular) for federal office on
each report.  What are you asking me to lump together?
The $200.00 contribution on the 10/23/84 report to the
United Democratic Campaign of San Mateo County (of
which I am also treasurer) cannot be broken down be-
tween federal and state candidates.  This committee
operated a storefront in the City of San Mateo ser-
vicing all Democratic Party Candidates (both state
and federal) appearing on the November 1984 ballot in
San Mateo County.  It did not make any contributions
to or expenditures for specific individual candidates.
It operated independently of any other committee.  It
is registered with and submits reports to both the
Federal Election Commission and the California Secretary
of State.

     6.  In my opinion the amendment for which you are asking
does not serve any constructive purpose and simply
creates unnecessary paperwork.  An amendment which
provides previously omitted data and/or corrects pre-
vious erroneous data serves a useful purpose.  On the
other hand we should not clutter the system with amend-
ments which, from a practical standpoint, simply advise
the reader of the amendment to disregard a small amount
of extra information on a previous document which must
remain on file anyway.

     7.  Thank you for your information relative to the Federal
Election Commission.  As previously noted I am also
treasurer of a committee registered with the FEC, so I
am generally family with their rules.  The 20th A/D
Democratic Committee has never reached the threshold
requiring it to registered with and report to the FEC.

I have no particular objection to filing these amendments
if you will make it clear what you are asking for.

Please, however, give some thought to the points raised
in this letter.

[signature]
Douglas DeYoung, Treasurer
20th Assembly District
Democratic Committee

P. S.  My phone number is 415-592-0888.

March Fong Eu
Secretary of State

1230 J Street
P O Box 1467
Sacramento, California 95807

POLITICAL REFORM DIVISION
(916) 322-4880

January 16, 1985

Mr. Douglas DeYoung, Treasurer
Twentieth Assembly District Democratic
Committee, ID# 801476
888 Holly Road
Belmont, CA 94002

Dear Mr. DeYoung:

Thank you for filing your Form 450, executed on October 23, 1984, for the reporting period
July 1, 1984 through October 20, 1984. As required by the Political Reform Act of 1974,
we have reviewed your statement to determine whether it conforms on its face with the
disclosure requirements.

We have noted the following to assist you in complying with the requirements of the law.

Disclosure of Contributions to Candidates for Federal Office

Contributions to candidates for federal office should be disclosed in a lump sum on
Section I, "Expenditures and Contributions Made To." To determine any duties you
may have under the Federal Election Campaign Act, I recommend you contact the
Federal Election Commission at 1325 K Street, N.W., Washington, D.C. 20463; toll
free (800) 424-9530.

Enclosed is an Amendment to Campaign Disclosure Statement, which should be completed
and filed to reflect any changes to your original campaign statement. Copies of your
amendment should be filed with all offices that received copies of the statement originally
filed. Please reply within fifteen days to this request.

If you have any questions, please call Gloria Gilmore at (916) 322-0395.

Sincerely,

MARIAN ASH, Assistant Chief
Political Reform Division

Enclosure

Mr. Douglas DeYoung, Treasurer
Twentieth Assembly District Democratic
Committee, ID# 801476
888 Holly Road
Belmont, CA 94002


Dear Mr. DeYoung:

Thank you for filing your Form 450, executed on July 28, 1984, for the reporting period
January 1, 1984 through June 30, 1984. As required by the Political Reform Act of 1974,
we have reviewed your statement to determine whether it conforms on its face with the
disclosure requirements.

We have noted the following to assist you in complying with the requirements of the law.

Disclosure of Contributions to Candidates for Federal Office

Contributions to candidates for federal office should be disclosed in a lump sum on
Section 1, "Expenditures and Contributions Made To." To determine any duties you
may have under the Federal Election Campaign Act, I recommend you contact the
Federal Election Commission at 1325 K Street, N.W., Washington, D.C. 20463; toll
free (800) 424-9530.

Enclosed is an Amendment to Campaign Disclosure Statement, which should be completed
and filed to reflect any changes to your original campaign statement. Copies of your
amendment should be filed with all offices that received copies of the statement originally
filed. Please reply within fifteen days to this request.

If you have any questions, please call Gloria Gilmore at (916) 322-0395.

Sincerely,


MARIAN ASH, Assistant Chief
Political Reform Division

Enclosure

ANSWER: 
Contributions to fedenal candidates are not required to 
be itemized but rather disclosed on Schedule E in a 
lump sum witn a notation that the expenditures represent 
contributions to federal candidates. 


RE: 
TWENTIETH ASSEMBLY DISTRICT DEMOCRATIC COMMITTEE ID# 801476 
888 Holly Road 
Belmont CA 94002

Political Reform Division -

Dear Ms. Ash & Ms. Gilmore: 
This acknowledges you letters of January 16 & 17 
(copies attached) requesting amendments to my 
campaign disclosure statements of June 28 and 
October 23, 1984. 
Your letter states in part: 
Contributions to candidates for federal office 
should be disclosed in a lump sum on Section I, 
"Expenditures and Contributions Made To:" 
I have several questions, comments, and suggestions 
concerning this. 
1. 
I cannot find the above statement in any of my 
reference documents pertaining to the preparation 
of the report. 
I'm not saying it doesn't appear. 
I just can't find it. 
Please advise where it 
appears. 
2. 
The statement is inconsistent with the general 
rule that a transaction or series of transactions 
cumulating $100.00 or more in a calendar year with 
a given entity must be itemized. 
Why are you 
making this exception? 
3. 
I have 0een under the impression that transactions 
between committees should be itemized regardless of 
the amount involved. 
Most treasurers operate under 
this assumption. 
4. 
I have been under the impression that while treasurers 
must not fail to itemize those transactions which are 
required to be itemized, this does not mean that trea-
surers must not itemize transactions which are not 
required to be itemized. 
We often desire to make in-
formation available to the general public above and 


beyond that required by law. 
In this case the 20th 
Assembly District Democratic Committee wishes to make 
its financial support to Martin Carnoy a matter of 
public record. 
Treasurers often like to itemize con-
tributions of less than $100.00 in order to demonstrate 
a broad base of local support or highlight some other 
point. 
I realize that there must be a practical limit 
to this so that required itemized transactions are not 
lost in a mass of trivial detail and reports do not 
become too bulky, but most treasurers won't go to such 
excesses anyway. 
5. 
I'm not sure exactly what you are asking me to do in 
this particular situation. 
Your letters refer to 
"candidates" (plural) for federal office. 
There is 
only one candidate (singular) for federal office on 
each report. 
What are you asking me to lump together? 
The $200.00 contribution on the 10/23/84 report to the 
United Democratic Campaign of San Mateo County (of 
which I am also treasurer) cannot be broken down be-
tween federal and state candidates. 
This committee 
operated a storefront in the City of San Mateo ser-
vicing all Democratic Party Candidates (both state 
and federal) appearing on the November 1984 ballot in 
San Mateo County. 
It did not make any contributions 
to or expenditures for specific individual candidates. 
It operated independently of any other committee. 
It 
is registered with and submits reports to both the 
Federal Election Commission and the California Secretary 
of State. 
6. 
In my opinion the amendment for which you are asking 
does not serve any constructive purpose and simply 
creates unnecessary paperwork. 
An amendment which 
p"rovides previously omitted data and/or corrects pre-
vious erroneous data serves a useful purpose. 
On the 
other hand we should not clutter the system with amend-
ments which, from a practical standpoint, simply advise 
the reader of the amendment to disregard a small amount 
of extra information on a previous document which must 
remain on file anyway. 
7. 
Thank you for your information relative to the Federal 
Election Commission. 
As previously noted I am also 
treasurer of a committee registered with the FEC, so I 
am generally family with their rules. 
The 20th A/D 
Democratic Committee has never reached the threshhold 
requiring it to registered with and report to the FEC. 


I have no particular objection to filing these amendments 
if you will make it clear what you are asking for. 
Please, however, give some 
in this letter. 
20th Assembly 
Democratic Committee

My phone number 1S 415-592-0888.

Federal Election Commission at 1325 K Street, N. W., Washington, D.C. 20463; toll 
free (800) 424-9530. 
Enclosed is an Amendment to Campaign Disclosure Statement, which should be completed 
and filed to reflect any Changes to your original campaign statement. Copies of your 
amendment should be filed with all offices that received copies of the statement originally 
filed. Please reply within fifteen days to this request.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: .How---t:G reoort contributions made b., the 20th Assembly 
District Democratic Committee to federal candidates. 
ANSWER: 
Contributions to fedenal candidates are not required to 
be itemized but rather disclosed on Schedule E in a 
lump sum witn a notation that the expenditures represent 
contributions to federal candidates. 


RE: 
TWENTIETH ASSEMBLY DISTRICT DEMOCRATIC COMMITTEE ID# 801476 
888 Holly Road 
Belmont CA 94002 
January 31, 1985 
Ms. Marian Ash/Ms. Gloria Gilmore 
Political Reform Division -
Secretary of 
P. O. Box 1467 
Sacramento, CA 95807 
Dear Ms. Ash & Ms. Gilmore: 
This acknowledges you letters of January 16 & 17 
(copies attached) requesting amendments to my 
campaign disclosure statements of June 28 and 
October 23, 1984. 
Your letter states in part: 
Contributions to candidates for federal office 
should be disclosed in a lump sum on Section I, 
"Expenditures and Contributions Made To:" 
I have several questions, comments, and suggestions 
concerning this. 
1. 
I cannot find the above statement in any of my 
reference documents pertaining to the preparation 
of the report. 
I'm not saying it doesn't appear. 
I just can't find it. 
Please advise where it 
appears. 
2. 
The statement is inconsistent with the general 
rule that a transaction or series of transactions 
cumulating $100.00 or more in a calendar year with 
a given entity must be itemized. 
Why are you 
making this exception? 
3. 
I have 0een under the impression that transactions 
between committees should be itemized regardless of 
the amount involved. 
Most treasurers operate under 
this assumption. 
4. 
I have been under the impression that while treasurers 
must not fail to itemize those transactions which are 
required to be itemized, this does not mean that trea-
surers must not itemize transactions which are not 
required to be itemized. 
We often desire to make in-
formation available to the general public above and 


beyond that required by law. 
In this case the 20th 
Assembly District Democratic Committee wishes to make 
its financial support to Martin Carnoy a matter of 
public record. 
Treasurers often like to itemize con-
tributions of less than $100.00 in order to demonstrate 
a broad base of local support or highlight some other 
point. 
I realize that there must be a practical limit 
to this so that required itemized transactions are not 
lost in a mass of trivial detail and reports do not 
become too bulky, but most treasurers won't go to such 
excesses anyway. 
5. 
I'm not sure exactly what you are asking me to do in 
this particular situation. 
Your letters refer to 
"candidates" (plural) for federal office. 
There is 
only one candidate (singular) for federal office on 
each report. 
What are you asking me to lump together? 
The $200.00 contribution on the 10/23/84 report to the 
United Democratic Campaign of San Mateo County (of 
which I am also treasurer) cannot be broken down be-
tween federal and state candidates. 
This committee 
operated a storefront in the City of San Mateo ser-
vicing all Democratic Party Candidates (both state 
and federal) appearing on the November 1984 ballot in 
San Mateo County. 
It did not make any contributions 
to or expenditures for specific individual candidates. 
It operated independently of any other committee. 
It 
is registered with and submits reports to both the 
Federal Election Commission and the California Secretary 
of State. 
6. 
In my opinion the amendment for which you are asking 
does not serve any constructive purpose and simply 
creates unnecessary paperwork. 
An amendment which 
p"rovides previously omitted data and/or corrects pre-
vious erroneous data serves a useful purpose. 
On the 
other hand we should not clutter the system with amend-
ments which, from a practical standpoint, simply advise 
the reader of the amendment to disregard a small amount 
of extra information on a previous document which must 
remain on file anyway. 
7. 
Thank you for your information relative to the Federal 
Election Commission. 
As previously noted I am also 
treasurer of a committee registered with the FEC, so I 
am generally family with their rules. 
The 20th A/D 
Democratic Committee has never reached the threshhold 
requiring it to registered with and report to the FEC. 


I have no particular objection to filing these amendments 
if you will make it clear what you are asking for. 
Please, however, give some 
in this letter. 
20th Assembly 
Democratic Committee 
P. S. 
My phone number 1S 415-592-0888. 


r 
J 
P 
Box 467 
1985

you for filing your 
450, executed on October 23, 1984, for the reporting period 
July 1, 1984 through October 20, 1984. As 
by the Political Reform Act of 1974, 
we have reviewed 
statement to determine whether it conforms on 1ts face with the 
We have noted 
following to assist you in 
with the requirements of the law.

Section I,

may have under the Federal Election Campaign Act, I recommend you contact the

Enclosed is an Amendment to Campaign Disclosure Statement, which should be completed

embedding.first_500_words: State of California Fair Political Practices Commission P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 Technical Assistance •• Administration •• Executive/Legal •• Enforcement (916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441 July 31, 1985 Douglas DeYoung, Treasurer 20th Assembly District Democratic Committee 888 Holly Road Belmont, CA 94002 Re: FPPC Advice No. A-85-150 Dear Mr. DeYoung: Your letter of January 31, 1985, addressed to the Secretary of State's office was referred to the Fair Political Practices Commission for response on July 2, 1985. I apologize for the delay in responding to your questions. Government Code Section 84211(j) does require contributions of $100 or more be itemized on campaign statements providing "...the date of the contribution, the cumulative amount of contributions made to a candidate, elected officer or committee, the full name of the candidate..." However, the Commission's staff has determined that contributions made to federal candidates are not required to be itemized but rather may be disclosed in a lump sum with a notation that the expenditure represents contributions to federal candidates. The rationale for this is that federal candidates are required under the federal election laws to disclose all contributions they receive on FEC campaign forms which are also filed with the Secretary of State's office. To require the itemization pursuant to the Political Reform Act of contributions of $100 or more made to federal candidates does not further the intent of the Act because anyone wishing to see what contributions a federal candidate has received can do so by looking at the FEC campaign statements. The letters dated January 16 and 17, 1985, from the Secretary of State's office are requesting that you amend the 450s filed by the 20th Assembly District Democratic Committee because these statements itemized contributions to federal candidates. I am asking the Secretary of State's office to reconsider their request for the amendments covered in the letters of January 16 and 17, 1985. In the future, however, if you wish, you may report contributions to federal candidates in the manner described above. Douglas DeYoung July 31, 1985 Page 2 Also, as a reminder, you are only required to itemize contributions which cumulatively reach $100 within a calendar year. On your statement filed August 1, 1984, covering the period January 1 through June 30, 1984, you itemized two $50.00 contributions. Contributions under $100 may be reported on Line 2, Part IV-Summary, Expenditures Under $100 (Not Itemized). If you have additional questions, please do not hesitate to contact me. Sincerely, [signature] Mary Ann Kvasager Political Reform Consultant Technical Assistance and Analysis Division MAK:kt cc: Gloria Gilmore, Secretary of State's Office, Political Reform Division TO: [ ]JEANNE [ ] JAY ( ) DAN FROM:_____Kvasager_____ [ ]LYNN [ ] BARBARA ( ) JOHN K [ ]ROGER [ ] CARLA ( ) MARY ANN DATE:_____1/31/85_____ (we received from SOS [ ]HELEN [ ] OTHER______________ July 2, 1985.)
